title: sonchus vulgaris subsp. arvensis
intro: Sonchus vulgaris subsp. arvensis, commonly known as field sowthistle, is a subspecies of the plant Sonchus vulgaris and is characterized by its annual or biennial growth habit and presence in disturbed areas. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and inflammation. Field sowthistle has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exerting antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of infections. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
